dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 13 for chest pain at a residence in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Sam from EMS. What's going on today?
I'm having a 6/10 chest pain right over my sternum. It's like a stabbing feeling but gets better when I press on it.
When did your chest pain start, and have you experienced any other symptoms today?
It started a few minutes ago while I was talking to my boss. I don't have nausea or dizziness.
Have you had chest pain before, and do you take any medications?
No, I don't have a history of chest pain, and I don't take any medications.
You seem oriented and able to interact during the exam, which helps with your evaluation.
Your oxygen saturation is 100% on room air.
Your blood pressure is 144 over 90, pulse is 70, and your breathing rate is 16.
We were unable to get a reliable blood sugar reading at this time.
Your trachea is midline and there's no evidence of jugular venous distension.
The chest wall is intact and not painful to touch in any area.
Lung sounds are clear and transmitted equally on both sides.
Peripheries are present with +PMS to all four limbs.
Abdomen is soft, not tender, and without abnormal distention.
Skin is warm, dry, and intact, with no observable rashes or trauma.
You have a history of fibromyalgia but no known allergies or medications.
You are maintaining your airway effectively and there is no sign of obstruction.
I'm going to place you on a cardiac monitor to evaluate your heart activity.
We've placed you on 4-lead ECG monitoring now.
A 12-lead EKG was obtained and shows a normal sinus rhythm for a woman your age.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light.
You remain alert and are answering questions clearly and consistently.
Your signs match behavioral and pain issues. We're following medical chest pain protocol.
You declined to go to the hospital but accepted the monitoring care. Did you sign the form?
Yes, I accept the risks and will follow up with my doctor later.
I've recorded your refusal and will leave the documents with you.
No rashes, scars, or trauma observed during the skin check.
You mentioned work today-had you experienced stress there?
I am just really stressed at work today. No other issues but this.
Your airway is patent, with no signs of obstruction or abnormal breath sounds.
Your respirations are 16 breaths per minute and remain calm and unlabored.
Pulse is 70, regular, and easy to palpate. Skin is warm and dry.
You opened your eyes spontaneously when I approached.
Your speech is clear and your answers are to the point without hesitation.
You respond to directions well and move freely with no hesitation.
No signs of drainage or foreign bodies in ears, nose, or mouth.
Heart sounds are normal without added murmurs or clicks.
Spine and back are intact with no pain on inspection or palpation.
We found no abnormalities in the genitourinary system.
Skin remains intact with no abnormal rashes or signs of insect bites.
No current medications reported, and there are no known allergies.
No signs of insect bites or skin trauma in the inspection.
You declined transport but agree to monitoring. Please consult with your physician soon.
Tell me the moment you first felt the pain.
The pain came on quickly during a conversation with my boss-sharp and stabbing.
Has the pain changed at all or stayed consistent since it began?
It has stayed steady at 6/10. I just feel very stressed about the whole thing.
I'd like to recheck your blood pressure and oxygen saturation for a second time.
Will confirm your vitals and monitor status again to be consistent.
We will continue monitoring on the 4-lead rhythm strip.
Avoiding further ECGs unless you experience a significant change.
What's your mood and activity like before the pain started?
I had a difficult meeting, and work has been stressful this whole week.
How are your symptoms now? Has there been any difference since we started?
Still 6/10. It doesn't feel worse but is not going away.
Joints and extremities are symmetrical with no deformities or pain.
You described fibromyalgia-how is this chest pain different?
This is newer and more localized. It doesn't match my usual pain patterns.
Your refusal and consent for monitoring are all recorded in the patient report.
You accepted care and monitoring but still declined hospital transport.
You can grasp and move each limb freely, and have no signs of restriction.
Breathing remains unlabored, with no signs of distress or accessory use.
Pulse is strong, regular, and peripheral, with warm extremities and capillary refill under 2 seconds.
What were you doing exactly when the pain began?
I just started to speak to my boss when the sharp pain hit right over my sternum.
No signs of neurologic deficits were found in the exam.
Have you noticed any shortness of breath with this chest pain?
No, I have full chest expansion and not real tightness at all.
I'll retake vitals one more time just to confirm they're stable again.
EKG remains unchanged, still showing a normal sinus rhythm pattern.
I'll guide you back safely to rest and provide final instruction.
I'll hand you the form for this day's refusal and keep a copy in the report.
Head tilt-chin lift position is not needed-your airway is patent and suitable for breathing.
Have you had similar episodes from stress or strain before?
No, this is my first time. I usually have headaches but not chest injuries.
Feel free to reach out if you need any help managing symptoms later.
I'll wrap up the gear here after this stay on location.
Final check-how do you feel right now with awareness of your symptoms?
Same level, but I'll definitely reach out to my provider if I don't improve.
Thank you for being honest today. Watch for any new or worsening symptoms.
I'll disconnect the monitoring now.
Skin remains consistent-no signs of petechiae, purpura, or trauma found.
Head and neck are normal with no palpable masses or fixation.
Limbs show no asymmetry and remain normal on physical inspection.
Rest is recommended today-reach out again if symptoms worsen or recur.
The assessment is complete and your refusal is clearly documented in reports.
No signs of circulation compromise. Peripheral pulses continue normal and equal.
Who is best to contact in the care of your condition after today?
My main doctor, I can contact him and explain this chest pain.
Don't hesitate to get help again if anything new or concerning happens.
Take the rest of the day to clear your head and reduce stress.
You did well in keeping calm and cooperating during the whole evaluation.
You were aware of each step and choice throughout the process.
The patient care report includes a decision for an AMA as per EPCR.
We provided a copy of the refusal form for your own documentation.
